questions.prompt: What is the main function of mitochondria?
questions.explain: Mitochondria generate ATP via cellular respiration — they're the powerhouse of the cell!
DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) encodes the genetic blueprint of all living organisms.

questions.prompt: What process do plants use to make food?
questions.explain: Photosynthesis converts sunlight + CO₂ + water into glucose and oxygen.

questions.prompt: Which organelle is responsible for protein synthesis?
questions.explain: Ribosomes translate mRNA into proteins — they can be free or attached to the ER.

questions.prompt: What type of bond holds the two DNA strands together?
questions.explain: Hydrogen bonds between base pairs (A-T, G-C) hold the double helix together.

questions.prompt: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explain: Mitochondria produce ATP through cellular respiration — classic biology fact!

questions.prompt: Which phase of mitosis comes first?
questions.explain: Prophase is the first stage — chromosomes condense and the spindle begins to form.

questions.prompt: What is the basic unit of life?
questions.explain: The cell is the fundamental structural and functional unit of all known life.
